name: Abdullah ibn Urwa
title: The Ghafari Brothers
shortDescription: A brave warrior from the Bani Ghafar tribe who, along with his brother Abd al-Rahman, stepped forward on Ashura with tears and eagerness for martyrdom.
fullStory:
Abdullah ibn Urwa al-Ghafari was a brave Shia and warrior from Kufa, belonging to the Bani Ghafar clan. He and his brother Abd al-Rahman were among those who, upon hearing the news of Imam Hussain's (AS) presence in Karbala, quickly reached his caravan to wield their swords for the Prophet's son.

On the day of Ashura, when the flames of war escalated and the Imam's companions were martyred one after another, Abdullah and his brother Abd al-Rahman came to Imam Hussain (AS). They stood before the Imam weeping.

Imam Hussain (AS) asked: 'O my nephews! Why are you crying? By God, I hope your eyes will soon be brightened'. They replied: 'May God sacrifice us for you! We are not crying for ourselves; our tears are because we see you surrounded by the enemy and we cannot defend you with more than our lives'.

After this loving expression of loyalty, Abdullah and his brother entered the battlefield with Imam Hussain's (AS) blessing. They recited war poetry in the field: 'The Bani Ghafar clan knows well that we have come to your aid with sharp swords'.

These two brothers fought shoulder to shoulder with utmost bravery. They fought so cohesively that whenever one was surrounded, the other rescued him. Finally, after killing a group of enemies, both were martyred next to each other.
quoteArabic: قَدْ عَلِمَتْ حَقّاً بَنُو غِفارِ، لَنَضْرِبَنَّ بِقَطْعِ الاَبْتارِ
quote: The Bani Ghafar know well that we have come to aid with sharp swords.
